summary: Machine policy for bypassing the lock screen. The local Microsoft ControlPanelDisplay.admx file documents the same Personalization policy key and NoLockScreen value used by the app.

setting.name: Lock Screen
setting.casual_explanation: Windows normally shows a lock screen before the sign-in screen. This policy decides whether that lock screen appears.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable the lock screen to get to sign-in faster and remove an extra click or swipe before credentials.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling it speeds up the path to sign-in, but it removes the lock-screen experience and its information surface.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents the NoLockScreen policy through the ADMX_ControlPanelDisplay Policy CSP and maps it to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization\NoLockScreen. The public documentation describes the enabled policy state and the not-configured or disabled baseline behavior, but it does not publish a raw DWORD value table, so this record models the documented enforced state plus the default unset state.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows shows the lock screen normally because the policy is not being forced.

allowed_values.label: Do not display the lock screen
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is enabled and users go straight to the sign-in experience instead of seeing the lock screen first.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes NoLockScreen = 1, which matches the documented enabled policy state.

windows_defaults.label: Windows machine-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 version 2004 with KB5005101 and later, plus Windows 11 systems that expose ADMX_ControlPanelDisplay

states.rationale: Microsoft documents the normal lock-screen behavior when the policy is disabled or not configured.

windows_defaults.notes: The default behavior keeps the lock screen in front of sign-in.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems that use the normal lock-screen experience
avoid_for: Users who want the fastest path to sign-in

states.rationale: Leave the lock screen behavior under Windows control.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the standard Windows lock-screen layer and its visuals.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to skip the lock screen entirely.
tradeoffs: The normal lock-screen experience remains available.
Users still need to dismiss it before sign-in.

recommended_profiles.label: Fast sign-in path
intended_for: Users who want one less sign-in step
avoid_for: Users who want the full lock-screen experience

states.rationale: Do not display the lock screen before sign-in.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to reach sign-in without stopping at the lock screen.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want lock-screen imagery and glanceable lock-screen information to remain available.
tradeoffs: The path to sign-in is more direct.
The lock-screen layer is removed by policy.

evidence.title: Microsoft ADMX_ControlPanelDisplay Policy CSP: NoLockScreen
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the scope, behavior, supported OS versions, and registry mapping for the NoLockScreen policy, including the enabled policy behavior and the not-configured or disabled baseline behavior.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes NoLockScreen = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: The local Microsoft ControlPanelDisplay.admx file explicitly defines NoLockScreen under Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization, and the official ControlPanelDisplay policy documentation describes the enabled behavior of skipping the lock screen. The app writes that exact enabled state.

validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file names the same machine policy surface used by the app. The official ADMX_ControlPanelDisplay policy documentation describes the enabled state as bypassing the lock screen.
